title: mentha aquatica var. agardhiana
intro: Mentha aquatica var. agardhiana, also known as water mint, is a variant of the water mint plant, a type of herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to ease digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Mentha aquatica var. agardhiana include relief from headaches, nausea, and menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, water mint is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin irritations.
